column1: 2 ^ Confitēbor tibī
column2: Domine
column3: in tōtō corde meō~; narrābō omnia mīrābilia tua. ^ I will give praise to thee
column5: with my whole heart~; I will relate all thy wonders.

column1: 3 ^ Laetābor et exsultābō in tē~; psallem nōminī tuō
column2: Altissime
column3: ^ I will be glad
column4: and rejoice in thee~; I will sing to thy name

column1: 4 ^ In convertendō inimīcum meum retrorsum~; infirmābuntur et perībunt ā faciē tuā. ^ When my enemy shall be turned back~; they shall be weakened and perish before thy face.
5 ^ Quoniam fēcistī jūdicium meum et causam meam~; sēdistī super tronum quī jūdicās jūstitiam. ^ For thou hast maintained my judgment and my cause~; thou hast sat on the throne
column2: who judgest justice.

column1: 6 ^ Increpāvistī Gentēs
column2: et perīvit impius~; nōmen eōrum dēlēvistī in aeternum et in saeculum saeculī. ^ Thou hast rebuked the Gentiles
column3: and the wicked one hath perished~; thou hast blotted out their name for ever and ever.

column1: 7 ^ Inimīcī dēfēcērunt frameae in fīnem
column2: et cīvitātēs eōrum dēstruxistī. Perīvit memoria eōrum cum sonitū~; ^ The swords of the enemy have failed unto the end
column3: and their cities thou hast destroyed. Their memory hath perished with a noise~;

column1: 8 ^ et Dominus in aeternum permanet. Parāvit in jūdiciō tronum suum
column2: ^ but the Lord remaineth for ever. He hath prepared his throne in judgment

column1: 9 ^ et ipse jūdicābit orbem terrae in aequitāte~; jūdicābit populōs in jūstitiā. ^ and he shall judge the world in equity~; he shall judge the people in justice.
10 ^ Et factus est Dominus refugium pauperī~; adjūtor in opportūnitātibus
column2: in trībulātiōne. ^ And the Lord is become a refuge for the poor~; a helper in due time in tribulation.

column1: 11 ^ Et spērent in tē quī nōvērunt nōmen tuum
column2: quoniam nōn dērelīquistī quaerentēs tē
column3: Domine. ^ And let them trust in thee who know thy name
column4: for thou hast not forsaken them that seek thee

column1: 12 ^ Psallite Dominō quī habitat in Siōne~; adnuntiem inter Gentēs studia ejus
column2: ^ Sing ye to the Lord
column3: who dwelleth in Sion~; declare his ways among the Gentiles

column1: 13 ^ quoniam requīrens sanguinem eōrum recordātus est~; nōn est oblītus clāmōrem pauperum. ^ for requiring their blood
column2: he hath remembered them~; he hath not forgotten the cry of the poor.

column1: 14 ^ Miserēre meī
column2: Domine~; vidē humilitātem meam dē inimīcīs meīs
column3: ^ Have mercy on me
column4: O Lord~; see my humiliation which I suffer from my enemies

column1: 15 ^ quī exaltās mē dē portīs mortis
column2: ut adnuntiem omnēs laudātiōnēs tuās in portīs fīliae Siōnis~; ^ thou that liftest me up from the gates of death
column3: that I may declare all thy praises in the gates of the daughter of Sion~;

column1: 16 ^ exsultābō in salūtārī tuō. Infixae sunt Gentēs in interitū
column2: quem fēcērunt~; in laqueō istō
column3: quem abscondērunt comprehensus est pēs eōrum. ^ I will rejoice in thy salvation. The Gentiles have stuck fast in the destruction which they prepared~; their foot hath been taken in the very snare which they hid.

column1: 17 ^ Cognōscētur Dominus jūdicia faciens~; in operibus manuum suārum comprehensus est peccātor. ^ The Lord shall be known when he executeth judgments~; the sinner hath been caught in the works of his own hands.
18 ^ Convertantur peccātōrēs in infernum
column2: omnēs gentēs quae oblīvīscuntur Deum. ^ The wicked shall be turned into hell
column3: all the nations that forget God.

column1: 19 ^ Quoniam nōn in fīnem oblīviō erit pauperis~; patientia pauperum nōn perībit in fīnem. ^ For the poor man shall not be forgotten to the end~; the patience of the poor shall not perish for ever.
20 ^ Exsurge
column2: Domine~; nōn confortētur homō~; jūdicentur Gentēs in conspectū tuō. ^ Arise
column3: O Lord~; let not man be strengthened~; let the Gentiles be judged in thy sight.

column1: 21 ^ Constitue
column2: Domine
column3: lēgislātōrem super eōs
column4: ut sciant Gentēs quoniam hominēs sunt. ^ Appoint
column7: that the Gentiles may know themselves to be but men.

column1: 22 ^ ¿~Ut quid
column2: Domine
column3: recessistī longē~; dēspicis in opportūnitātibus
column6: hast thou retired afar off~; why dost thou slight us in our wants

column1: 23 ^ Dum superbit impius
column2: incenditur pauper~; comprehenduntur in consiliīs quibus cōgitant. ^ Whilst the wicked man is proud
column3: the poor is set on fire~; they are caught in the counsels which they devise.

column1: 24 ^ Quoniam laudātur peccātor in dēsīderiīs animae suae
column2: et inīquus benedīcitur. ^ For the sinner is praised in the desires of his soul
column3: and the unjust man is blessed.

column1: 25 ^ Exacerbāvit Dominum peccātor~; secundum multitūdinem īrae suae nōn quaeret~; nōn est Deus in conspectū ejus. ^ The sinner hath provoked the Lord~; according to the multitude of his wrath he will not seek him~; God is not before his eyes.

26 ^ Inquinātae sunt viae illīus in omnī tempore~; auferuntur jūdicia tua ā faciē ejus~; omnium inimīcōrum suōrum dominābitur. ^ His ways are filthy at all times~; thy judgments are removed from his sight~; he shall rule over all his enemies.
27 ^ Dixit enim in corde suō
column2: «~Nōn movēbor ā generātiōne in generātiōnem
column3: sine malō.~»^ For he hath said in his heart
column4: "I shall not be moved from generation to generation

column1: 28 ^ Cujus maledictiōne ōs plēnum est
column2: et amāritūdine
column3: et dolō~; sub linguā ejus labor et dolor. ^ His mouth is full of cursing
column5: and of deceit~; under his tongue are labor and sorrow.

column1: 29 ^ Sedet in insidiīs cum dīvitibus in occultīs
column2: ut interficiat innocentem. ^ He sits in ambush with the rich in private places
column3: that he may kill the innocent.

column1: 30 ^ Oculī ejus in pauperem respiciunt~; insidiātur in absconditō
column2: quasi leō in spēluncā suā. Insidiātur ut rapiat pauperem~; rapere pauperem dum attrahit eum. ^ His eyes are upon the poor man~; he lieth in wait in secret like a lion in his den. He lieth in ambush that he may catch the poor man~; to catch the poor
column3: whilst he draweth him to him.

column1: 31 ^ In laqueō suō humiliābit eum~; inclīnābit sē
column2: et cadet quum dominātus fuerit pauperum. ^ In his net he will bring him down~; he will bow himself down
column3: and fall when he shall have power over the poor.

column1: 32 ^ Dixit enim in corde suō
column2: «~Oblītus est Deus~; āvertit faciem suam
column3: nē videat in fīnem.~»^ For he hath said in his heart
column5: he hath turned away his face not to see to the end."

column1: 33 ^ Exsurge
column2: Domine Deus~; exaltētur manus tū~; nē oblīvīscāris pauperum. ^ Arise
column3: O Lord God~; let thy hand be exalted~; forget not the poor.

column1: 34 ^ ¿~Propter quid irrītāvit impius Deum~? Dixit enim in corde suō
column2: «~Nōn requīret.~»^ Wherefore hath the wicked provoked God~? For he hath said in his heart
column3: "He will not require it."

column1: 35 ^ Vidēs
column2: quoniam tū labōrem et dolōrem consīderās
column3: ut trādās eōs in manūs tuās. Tibī dērelictus est pauper~; orfanō tū eris adjūtor. ^ Thou seest it
column4: for thou considerest labour and sorrow
column5: that thou mayst deliver them into thy hands. To thee is the poor man left~; thou wilt be a helper to the orphan.

column1: 36 ^ Contere bracchium peccātōris et malignī~; quaerētur peccātum illīus
column2: et nōn inveniētur. ^ Break thou the arm of the sinner and of the malignant~; his sin shall be sought
column3: and shall not be found.

column1: 37 ^ Dominus regnābit in aeternum
column2: et in saeculum saeculī~; perībitis Gentēs dē terrā illīus. ^ The Lord shall reign to eternity
column3: yea
column4: for ever and ever~; ye Gentiles shall perish from his land.

column1: 38 ^ Dēsīderium pauperum exaudīvit Dominus~; praeparātiōnem cordis eōrum audīvit auris tua. ^ The Lord hath heard the desire of the poor~; thy ear hath heard the preparation of their heart.
39 ^ Jūdicāre pūpillō et humilī
column2: ut nōn appōnat ultrā magnificāre sē homō super terram. ^ To judge for the fatherless and for the humble
column3: that man may no more presume to magnify himself upon earth.
